decisions.title: Prioritize Canvas Pass/Fail Functions vs Explore V2 Family Stats DnD
decisions.context: Both Canvas (ENG-9232) and Explore V2 (ENG-10404) have polish features in flight. Canvas is 'Build the Moat' territory; Explore is 'Nail the Core'. With PyRunner production issues (ENG-9056) unresolved, Canvas execution reliability is at risk.

arguments.for: Explore V2 drives immediate North Star impact—more Decisions Captured via better family workflows. Canvas is differentiated but not yet critical path.
arguments.against: Canvas Pass/Fail is foundational for moat-building; delaying risks competitive parity. PyRunner issues affect both products equally.

decisions.title: Resolve Sift Edge production blockers before Phase 2 rollout
decisions.context: Sift Edge has GUI table view (ENG-9883), enum/units support (ENG-8857), and transaction metrics (ENG-10403) in flight, but compaction issues (ENG-10308, ENG-10371) cause extreme fragmentation and disk space failures. Edge is 'Embed Deeper' pillar—customers expect stability.

arguments.for: Stability blockers undermine customer trust and adoption velocity. Fix before expanding surface area.
arguments.against: GUI and metrics features are customer-visible wins that demonstrate progress; can ship behind feature flags.

decisions.title: Gate Python Rules GA on PyRunner prod availability SLA
decisions.context: Python Rules is a 2026 strategic bet ('Build the Moat'), but PyRunner pods are unavailable in prod (ENG-9056). No SLA defined. UDF dev experience also broken (ENG-10396). Risk: GA launch without production reliability.

arguments.for: Defining SLA forces engineering prioritization and prevents premature GA that damages reputation.
arguments.against: Customers are waiting; can launch beta with clear disclaimers and iterate on reliability.

decisions.title: Invest in Ask SIFT conversation state machine now or defer to H2
decisions.context: ENG-10385 (conversation state machine) is foundational for Ask SIFT multi-turn interactions—key to 'Signal>Evidence>Decision' loop. But competes with core product debt (compaction, PyRunner, SCIM).

arguments.for: State machine unlocks differentiated conversational workflows; delaying risks Ask SIFT becoming a parlor trick vs. strategic product.
arguments.against: Core products (Explore, Canvas, Edge) have higher immediate North Star impact. Ask SIFT can iterate without state machine in Q2.

decisions.title: Address CSRS shadow forwarding bypass before expanding data-batcher throughput
decisions.context: ENG-10257 (CSRS bypass) means worker queries skip critical security headers. ENG-10402 (data-batcher throughput) increases load. Risk: scaling broken auth path.

arguments.for: Security issue could expose customer data at scale; must fix before increasing throughput.
arguments.against: Throughput bottleneck is customer-facing (slow queries); can mitigate CSRS risk with monitoring while scaling.

keyResults.name: Explore V2: 80% of MAUs use family workflows
Canvas: 50% of Explore users create 1+ canvas/mo
Compaction performance: <2hr p95 at gov scale
Sift Edge: 30 active deployments by Q2
Python Rules: 10 customers in GA by EOQ2
Ask SIFT: 40% of users query 1+ time/week
Canvas Pass/Fail: 25% of canvases use functions

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy week: 5 critical decisions (Canvas vs Explore priority, Edge stability gate, PyRunner SLA, Ask SIFT state machine, CSRS security). 22 meetings = 80% of time. Only 8 free hours for deep work. Risk: decision latency on Python Rules GA and Edge rollout.
alerts: 80% meeting load—consider delegating Edge stability review to eng lead
3 consecutive 1:1s Wed PM—buffer needed for context-switching
No focus blocks Thu/Fri—consider blocking 2hr windows for decision docs
PyRunner GA decision overdue by 1 week—escalate to eng leadership
CSRS security issue needs exec visibility—add to Friday leadership sync
